generated.tagline: A small Williamsburg coffee bar with toast and time
generated.aboutP1: Larkspur sits on a quiet corner of Bedford, with afternoon light through the front window and a counter that fits four. Mornings are quick — laptops, espresso, the line out the door before nine. After eleven the room slows down and people stay.
generated.aboutP2: The menu stays narrow on purpose: a few good toasts, a few good drinks, and a small case of pastries from a friend in Greenpoint. Service is unhurried once the rush has passed. Stop in mid-week, take a small table by the window, and stay for a while.

signatureDishes.name: Cardamom Bun
signatureDishes.line: A long, slow-rolled morning bun with cardamom sugar and citrus zest

signatureDishes.name: Hot Honey Toast
signatureDishes.line: Country bread, fresh ricotta, hot honey, sea salt — eaten standing if it is busy

signatureDishes.name: Single-Origin Drip
signatureDishes.line: Three rotating Brooklyn-roasted beans, brewed one cup at a time
